name: Apple Shortcuts Runner
description: Finds and triggers macOS Apple Shortcuts entirely from the command line — no UI interaction. Lists installed shortcuts, searches them by name, and runs a shortcut by name with optional text/file input and captured output. Uses the built-in 'shortcuts' CLI (macOS 12+) and automatically falls back to AppleScript (osascript) when the CLI is unavailable.
author: custom

examples.prompt: Find shortcuts with 'note' in the name
Run the 'Add to Inbox' shortcut with the input 'Buy milk'
Run 'Resize Image' on the file at /tmp/photo.jpg and save the result to /tmp/out.jpg

action.description: Action to perform: 'list' (list all installed shortcuts), 'find' (filter shortcuts whose name contains 'query'), or 'run' (trigger a shortcut by 'name').

name.description: Exact name of the shortcut to run. Required for action 'run'. If the name is not found, the skill returns close-matching suggestions instead of running anything.

query.description: Case-insensitive substring to match against shortcut names. Required for action 'find'.

input.description: Optional text passed as input to the shortcut (action 'run'). Mutually exclusive with 'inputPath'; if both are given, 'inputPath' wins.

inputPath.description: Optional path to a file passed as input to the shortcut (action 'run'). Takes precedence over 'input'. CLI engine only.

outputPath.description: Optional path to write the shortcut's output to (action 'run'). If omitted, output is written to a temp file and returned inline. CLI engine only.
